mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals labor market normalization, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding consumer credit balances indicate synchronized household spending and investment, which lifts aggregate demand and pushes S&P 500 forward returns higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample walk-forward testing, with a cumulative information coefficient below 0.05 over three years.

mechanism_narrative: An uptick in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and supports corporate profit margins, yielding positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no statistically significant positive skew when INDPRO rises month-over-month, with a t-statistic below 1.65.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth exceeds 2.5 percent, it confirms robust economic momentum that overrides short-term valuation concerns, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5 percent
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of quarters where GDP growth surpasses the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll growth and accelerating consumer price inflation create a wage-price spiral that forces aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces positive forward returns in the majority of out-of-sample periods, or the combined predictive power drops below a 0.02 Rank IC over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month increase in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for broader construction and durable goods demand, directly supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal yields a negative or statistically insignificant Sharpe ratio over a 10-year walk-forward test, with forward returns averaging below zero during rising housing periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent alongside a consumer price index above 300, tight monetary policy combined with elevated inflation crushes real corporate margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0 percent and CPIAUCSL exceeds 300
failure_condition: The joint threshold condition fails to predict negative returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample period.
